topic.what: In-vivo and in-vitro study demonstrating that Cheonwangbosim-dan (天王補心丹, a classical Korean/TCM heart-nourishing formula) exhibits anti-H. pylori activity and reduces gastric inflammation in both a cell model and animal model — representing an instance of formula repurposing from cardiovascular/neurological to gastric indications.
topic.how: Cheonwangbosim-dan aqueous extract tested for MIC against H. pylori in vitro; anti-H. pylori and anti-inflammatory effects assessed in H. pylori-infected gastric epithelial cells (cytokine expression) and in H. pylori-infected mouse model (histopathology, colonization density, inflammatory marker expression).
topic.why_matters: Cheonwangbosim-dan is a canonical multi-herb formula (containing Rehmannia, Asparagus, Schisandra, Platycladus, Ziziphus, and others) prescribed primarily for heart palpitations and insomnia in Korean traditional medicine; discovering anti-H. pylori activity represents formula repurposing and suggests that multi-herb formulas with cardiovascular and neuroprotective constituents may contain anti-gastric-pathogen activity as a secondary property.

characteristics.what_it_measures: MIC of Cheonwangbosim-dan extract against H. pylori
characteristics.value: Significant inhibitory activity against H. pylori reference strain
characteristics.vs_baseline: Clarithromycin positive control; vehicle negative control
characteristics.context: Crude formula extract; active phytochemicals not isolated

characteristics.dimension: H. pylori colonization reduction in vivo
characteristics.what_it_measures: H. pylori colonization density in mouse gastric mucosa after treatment
characteristics.value: Significant reduction in H. pylori colonization density in treated mice
characteristics.evidence: Quantitative culture and/or rapid urease test on gastric tissue
characteristics.context: Mouse H. pylori infection model; colonization density not full eradication

characteristics.what_it_measures: Inflammatory cell infiltration and cytokine expression in gastric mucosa
characteristics.value: Reduced NF-κB activation, IL-8, and inflammatory infiltrate in treated vs infected controls
characteristics.evidence: Histopathology (H&E), immunohistochemistry, or Western blot for inflammatory markers
characteristics.context: Anti-inflammatory effect may partially explain gastric benefit independent of direct antibacterial action

characteristics.what_it_measures: Degree of shift from traditional cardiovascular/neurological indication to gastric anti-infective use
characteristics.value: Primary formula indication is cardiovascular/neurological (Shim = heart); gastric activity is novel secondary discovery
characteristics.vs_baseline: Classical formula indication vs demonstrated gastric activity
characteristics.evidence: Traditional medical texts (Dongui Bogam) document formula for non-GI indications
characteristics.context: Formula repurposing pattern: multi-herb formulas may have pleotropic tissue effects beyond primary indication

good_for: Demonstrating multi-herb formula pleiotropy: cardiovascular formulas with anti-gastric-pathogen properties
Identifying Korean traditional formulas for H. pylori adjunct therapy
Screening other heart/nerve-nourishing formulas for unexpected anti-H. pylori activity
Concept generation: traditional formula repurposing strategy for novel anti-H. pylori agents
not_for: First-line H. pylori eradication therapy (evidence is early-stage, no clinical trials)
Patients with sedative herb sensitivity (formula contains Ziziphus, Schisandra with sedative properties)
Replacing triple/quadruple antibiotic therapy in confirmed H. pylori infection
requires: Identification of active anti-H. pylori components within the multi-herb formula
Clinical trials in H. pylori-positive gastritis patients
Drug interaction screening with standard H. pylori eradication regimens
Long-term safety assessment for extended use beyond traditional cardiovascular indication

novelty: First demonstration of anti-H. pylori and gastric anti-inflammatory activity for Cheonwangbosim-dan, a formula canonically used for cardiovascular/neurological indications
Provides a compelling example of Korean traditional formula repurposing: secondary pharmacological effects discovered by systematic testing beyond primary indication
Suggests that formulas with Ziziphus, Schisandra, and Rehmannia constituents may share anti-H. pylori properties worth systematic investigation
open_problems: Which constituent herb(s) or compound(s) carry the anti-H. pylori activity not determined
Whether full formula or subset of herbs is required for observed activity not tested
Eradication vs suppression vs colonization reduction distinction not fully characterized
Interaction with antibiotic-based triple therapy (additive, synergistic, or antagonistic) unknown
Optimal dosing and treatment duration for gastric indication not established
